title: RD53 Collaboration -- pixel readout chips for ATLAS/CMS Phase-2 HL-LHC upgrades
authors_or_org: CERN (RD53 Collaboration), with Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory contributions
claim_supported: CERN's own EP-ESE page reportedly confirms the RD53 collaboration (established 2013) designs pixel-readout ICs for ATLAS/CMS Phase-2 HL-LHC upgrades under extreme radiation (1 Grad) and hit-rate (3.5 GHz/cm^2) requirements, with LBNL contributing next-generation pixel-detector/readout-chip technology -- claim not independently confirmed by a fetched page this session.
locator: Both attempted CERN EP-ESE URLs (the content-path URL and a short /rd53 alias) redirected to a CERN single-sign-on authentication wall this session (auth.cern.ch); no public content could be retrieved.
rejection_reason: Inaccessible: CERN SSO auth-wall blocked both attempted URL variants this session; no public content could be retrieved. The RD53 collaboration's core technical facts (radiation tolerance, hit-rate targets) are independently covered by the verified peer-reviewed JINST record L13-009, so this rejection does not remove lane coverage of RD53 -- it removes only this specific institutional-framing page.
notes: Complements the JINST journal record L13-009; rejected as inaccessible rather than merged, since it would have offered institutional/programmatic framing (e.g., LBNL's specific role) beyond L13-009 that could not be confirmed this session.
